description: Add a reusable `ResetAsync` path to `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlGeneratedDdlTestDatabase.cs` so generated-DDL-backed fixtures can reuse one provisioned database instead of recreating it per test. Acceptance: `ResetAsync` truncates all data-bearing tables across `dms` and model schemas, restarts identity/sequence state required by the tests, preserves the provisioned schema, and can be called repeatedly without leaving stale rows or broken constraints.

Add a reusable `ResetAsync` path to `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlGeneratedDdlTestDatabase.cs` and implement it as a single batched reset operation rather than a per-table loop in each fixture. Acceptance: reset clears user data for `dms`, `edfi`, `auth`, and extension schemas, reseeds identity columns, restarts `dms.ChangeVersionSequence`, leaves schema objects intact, and is idempotent across repeated test runs.

Cache immutable fixture artifacts in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlGeneratedDdlFixtureLoader.cs`. Acceptance: repeated loads of the same fixture directory reuse the same effective schema, model set, and generated DDL build output; the cache is thread-safe for future parallel execution; and no existing PostgreSQL integration test mutates cached artifacts in place.

Cache immutable fixture artifacts in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlGeneratedDdlFixtureLoader.cs`. Acceptance: repeated loads of the same fixture directory reuse the same effective schema, model set, and generated DDL output; the cache is thread-safe; and no existing MSSQL integration test depends on mutating loader-returned objects.

Convert `PostgresqlReferenceResolverTests.cs` and `PostgresqlReferenceResolverTestDatabaseTests.cs` to provision the test database once per fixture and use `ResetAsync` plus `SeedAsync` for per-test isolation. Acceptance: these files no longer call `CreateProvisionedAsync()` from `[SetUp]`, the database is created and dropped once per fixture, and the reference resolver tests remain deterministic across repeated runs.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "\[SetUp\]|CreateProvisionedAsync\(" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlReferenceResolverTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlReferenceResolverTestDatabaseTests.cs` and confirm `CreateProvisionedAsync()` is not used from `[SetUp]`.

description: Convert `MssqlReferenceResolverTests.cs` and `MssqlReferenceResolverTestDatabaseTests.cs` to provision the test database once per fixture and use `ResetAsync` plus `SeedAsync` for per-test isolation. Acceptance: these files no longer call `CreateProvisionedAsync()` from `[SetUp]`, the database is created and dropped once per fixture, and the MSSQL reference resolver tests remain deterministic across repeated runs.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "\[SetUp\]|CreateProvisionedAsync\(" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlReferenceResolverTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlReferenceResolverTestDatabaseTests.cs` and confirm `CreateProvisionedAsync()` is not used from `[SetUp]`.

description: Convert the PostgreSQL generated-DDL smoke fixtures in `PostgresqlGeneratedDdlFocusedSmokeTests.cs`, `PostgresqlGeneratedDdlHarnessTests.cs`, and `PostgresqlGeneratedDdlAuthoritativeSmokeTests.cs` to one-time provisioning. Acceptance: none of these files creates and drops a database per test; fixture setup uses one provisioned database plus reset/seed as needed; and idempotent DDL reapply coverage remains in exactly one deliberate path per fixture or a dedicated test.

Convert the SQL Server generated-DDL smoke fixtures in `MssqlGeneratedDdlFocusedSmokeTests.cs`, `MssqlGeneratedDdlHarnessTests.cs`, and `MssqlGeneratedDdlAuthoritativeSmokeTests.cs` to one-time provisioning. Acceptance: none of these files creates and drops a database per test; fixture setup uses one provisioned database plus reset/seed as needed; and explicit idempotent DDL reapply coverage remains intentional and limited to one path per fixture or a dedicated test.

Convert `PostgresqlRelationalWriteGuardedNoOpTests.cs` to reuse one provisioned database per fixture or nested fixture instead of provisioning in each `[SetUp]`. Acceptance: every nested fixture in this file uses one-time provisioning plus reset/seed or immutable setup; no nested fixture recreates the database per test; and all guarded-no-op assertions still pass.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "\[SetUp\]|CreateProvisionedAsync\(" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlRelationalWriteGuardedNoOpTests.cs` and confirm database creation moved out of per-test setup.

description: Convert `PostgresqlRelationalWritePostAsUpdateSmokeTests.cs` to reuse one provisioned database per fixture or nested fixture instead of provisioning in each `[SetUp]`. Acceptance: every nested fixture in this file moves DB creation out of per-test setup, uses reset/seed or immutable one-time setup for isolation, and preserves the current post-as-update behavioral coverage.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "\[SetUp\]|CreateProvisionedAsync\(" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlRelationalWritePostAsUpdateSmokeTests.cs` and confirm database creation moved out of per-test setup.

description: Convert the `[SetUp]`-driven fixture in `PostgresqlRelationalWriteAuthoritativeSampleSmokeTests.cs` to one-time provisioning plus reset/seed, and align it with the file's existing `OneTimeSetUp` fixture pattern. Acceptance: the first fixture in the file no longer provisions the database in `[SetUp]`, test isolation is preserved, and both create/update/no-op assertions continue to pass.

Convert the smaller PostgreSQL write fixtures in `PostgresqlRelationalWriteCreateBaselineTests.cs`, `PostgresqlRelationalWriteUpdateSemanticsTests.cs`, `PostgresqlDescriptorWriteTests.cs`, and `PostgresqlReferentialIdentityTests.cs` to use one-time provisioning plus reset/seed. Acceptance: these files no longer create and drop a generated-DDL or resolver database per test, and their existing behavior-specific assertions still pass.

Convert the collection- and rollback-oriented PostgreSQL write fixtures in `PostgresqlRelationalWriteCollectionReorderTests.cs`, `PostgresqlRelationalWriteMultiBatchCollectionTests.cs`, and `PostgresqlRelationalWriteRollbackSafetyTests.cs` to use one-time provisioning plus reset/seed. Acceptance: these files stop provisioning a fresh database in each `[SetUp]`, retain isolation between tests, and preserve current collection ordering, batching, and rollback assertions.

Convert `MssqlRelationalWriteAuthoritativeSampleStudentSchoolAssociationSmokeTests.cs` to use one-time provisioning plus reset/seed instead of provisioning in `[SetUp]`. Acceptance: the file no longer recreates the database per test, retains deterministic fixture data setup, and all create/get/profile assertions still pass.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "\[SetUp\]|CreateProvisionedAsync\(" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlRelationalWriteAuthoritativeSampleStudentSchoolAssociationSmokeTests.cs` and confirm DB creation moved out of per-test setup.

description: Convert the remaining MSSQL write fixtures in `MssqlRelationalWriteAuthoritativeSampleStudentArtProgramAssociationSmokeTests.cs`, `MssqlRelationalWriteAuthoritativeDs52SurveySmokeTests.cs`, and `MssqlReferentialIdentityTests.cs` to use one-time provisioning plus reset/seed or immutable one-time setup. Acceptance: these files stop provisioning a fresh database per test and preserve current behavioral assertions.

Stop the fingerprint reader tests from forcing shared-database behavior. Acceptance: `PostgresqlDatabaseFingerprintReaderTests.cs` no longer mutates the configured shared PostgreSQL database directly, `MssqlDatabaseFingerprintReaderTests.cs` no longer creates and drops a full database in `[SetUp]`, and both fixtures use isolated minimal-schema provisioning or reset-based helpers that are safe to run alongside other isolated fixtures.
steps-to-verify: Run: `rg -n "Configuration\.DatabaseConnectionString|CreateProvisionedAsync\(" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlDatabaseFingerprintReaderTests.cs ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlDatabaseFingerprintReaderTests.cs` and confirm the per-test shared-db/provisioning patterns are removed.

description: Add a PostgreSQL baseline/template-database harness for high-cost generated-DDL fixtures and adopt it in one heavy PostgreSQL fixture. Acceptance: the helper provisions a baseline once per fixture signature, creates isolated test databases from that baseline, and at least one heavy fixture uses it without leaking state across tests or rerunning full DDL on every test case.

Add a SQL Server baseline restore harness, preferably snapshot-based, for high-cost generated-DDL fixtures and adopt it in one heavy MSSQL fixture. Acceptance: the helper can return a provisioned database to a known-good baseline without replaying all DDL for every test, and at least one heavy MSSQL fixture uses it successfully.

Audit `NonParallelizable` usage across both relational integration projects and enable NUnit parallel execution for fixtures that now use isolated databases or per-fixture baselines. Acceptance: converted isolated fixtures no longer carry `NonParallelizable`, assembly- or project-level parallel settings are added intentionally, and only truly shared-state fixtures remain serialized.
